title: Should Students Have Part-Time Jobs?
requirement: Some people believe that students should focus entirely on their studies, while others think part-time jobs are beneficial. Write an essay discussing both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
In recent years, whether students should take part-time jobs has been widely debated. In my opinion, working part-time brings more benefits than drawbacks.

On one hand, part-time jobs help students develop practical skills such as time management, communication, and financial literacy. These skills are rarely taught in classrooms but are essential for future careers. Moreover, earning their own money teaches students the value of hard work and helps them become more independent.

On the other hand, opponents argue that working takes time away from studying and may lead to declining academic performance. They also worry about students being exploited in the workplace.

However, I believe the key lies in balance. If students limit their working hours to no more than 15 per week and prioritize their studies, the benefits far outweigh the risks. Part-time work provides real-world experience that textbooks simply cannot offer.

In conclusion, with proper time management, part-time jobs can be a valuable complement to academic learning.
key_points: 兼职培养时间管理和沟通等实用技能
自己赚钱学会独立和珍惜劳动
关键在于平衡工作和学习
每周不超过15小时为宜
实践经验是课本无法替代的
tips: 开头用近年热点引入话题
正反两面论述使论证更全面
用具体数字(15小时)增强说服力
结尾总结并呼应开头
注意过渡词的使用：Moreover, However, In conclusion

title: Is Online Learning as Effective as Traditional Learning?
requirement: With the growth of online education, some people argue it is as effective as traditional classroom learning, while others disagree. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The rapid development of technology has made online learning increasingly popular. While some praise its flexibility, others question its effectiveness compared to traditional learning.

Supporters of online learning highlight its convenience and accessibility. Students can learn at their own pace and review materials anytime. It also removes geographical barriers, allowing learners to access quality education worldwide. During the pandemic, online learning proved essential for continuing education.

However, critics point out significant drawbacks. Online learners often lack face-to-face interaction, which is crucial for developing social skills and building relationships. Additionally, self-discipline is required, and many students struggle with distractions at home. Technical issues can also disrupt the learning process.

In my view, a blended approach combining both methods offers the best solution. Online learning excels at delivering content, while traditional classrooms foster collaboration and critical thinking. Together, they can provide a more comprehensive educational experience.

In conclusion, rather than replacing traditional learning, online education should complement it to maximize learning outcomes.
key_points: 在线学习灵活便捷，可自主安排节奏
但缺乏面对面互动和社交环境
自律要求高，在家易分心
混合式教学是最佳方案
在线和传统应互补而非替代
tips: 从科技发展引入话题很自然
支持方和反对方各用一段
给出自己的观点时用In my view
提出解决方案使论证更完整
用rather than...should...强调建议

title: Should College Education Be Free?
requirement: Some countries offer free college education while others charge high tuition fees. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of free college education and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The question of whether college education should be free has sparked heated debate globally. Both sides present compelling arguments.

Advocates of free college education argue that it promotes equal opportunity. Students from low-income families can access higher education without accumulating debt. A more educated population also benefits society through innovation and economic growth. Countries like Germany and Norway have demonstrated that tuition-free education is feasible.

Conversely, opponents contend that free education places a heavy burden on taxpayers. Without tuition revenue, universities may struggle to maintain quality facilities and attract top faculty. There is also concern that free education might reduce students' motivation, as they have less personal financial investment.

In my opinion, while completely free education may not be practical for every country, affordable education should be a priority. Governments could adopt a sliding-scale tuition system based on family income, ensuring that financial constraints never prevent talented students from pursuing higher education.

To sum up, accessibility and quality must both be considered when shaping education policy.
key_points: 免费教育促进机会平等
减少学生债务负担
但增加纳税人负担可能降低教育质量
可能降低学生动力
按收入阶梯收费是折中方案
tips: 用全球性话题开头吸引注意
举例德国和挪威增强说服力
提出折中方案显示思维深度
结尾强调两个目标都要兼顾
用sliding-scale等术语显示词汇量

title: The Impact of Social Media on Teenagers
requirement: Social media has become an integral part of teenagers' lives. Write an essay discussing the positive and negative effects of social media on teenagers. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Social media has revolutionized how teenagers communicate and access information. While it offers significant benefits, its negative effects cannot be ignored.

On the positive side, social media enables teenagers to connect with peers globally and express themselves creatively. It provides platforms for learning, collaboration, and social causes. Many young people have used social media to raise awareness about important issues like climate change and mental health.

However, the negative impacts are concerning. Studies link excessive social media use to anxiety, depression, and low self-esteem among teenagers. Cyberbullying has become a serious problem, and the constant comparison with others' curated lives creates unrealistic expectations. Additionally, excessive screen time reduces physical activity and face-to-face social interaction.

In my view, social media itself is neither good nor bad—it depends on how it is used. Parents and schools should educate teenagers about digital literacy and healthy online habits. Setting reasonable limits on screen time while encouraging offline activities can help teenagers enjoy the benefits while minimizing the harm.

Ultimately, responsible use is the key to a healthy relationship with social media.
key_points: 社交媒体连接全球同龄人，促进创意表达
过度使用导致焦虑、抑郁和低自尊
网络欺凌和攀比心理是严重问题
社交媒体本身中性，取决于使用方式
数字素养教育和健康习惯是关键
tips: 正反各一段结构清晰
用Studies link...引用研究增强可信度
指出问题本质（中性工具）显示深度
给出具体建议（数字素养教育）
结尾呼应论点简洁有力

title: Should Animals Be Kept in Zoos?
requirement: Some people believe zoos play an important role in conservation and education, while others argue they are cruel and should be abolished. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The debate over whether animals should be kept in zoos has continued for decades. Both sides raise valid points that deserve careful consideration.

Proponents argue that modern zoos serve vital conservation purposes. They protect endangered species through breeding programs and provide educational opportunities that inspire people to care about wildlife. Many zoos also fund habitat preservation projects in the wild, contributing directly to species survival.

Opponents, however, contend that confining animals is inherently cruel. Many zoo enclosures are too small to allow natural behaviors, leading to stress and health problems. They argue that animals should live in their natural habitats, and that conservation efforts should focus on protecting ecosystems rather than displaying animals for entertainment.

In my opinion, while the ideal scenario would be all animals living freely in protected habitats, well-regulated zoos currently serve an important purpose. The key is ensuring that zoos prioritize animal welfare over profit, provide spacious and naturalistic enclosures, and focus on genuine conservation work rather than mere exhibition.

Striking this balance is essential for the future of both zoos and wildlife.
key_points: 现代动物园有保护和教育功能
濒危物种繁殖计划有重要意义
但圈养限制动物自然行为
理想状态是动物生活在自然栖息地
动物园应优先福利而非利润
tips: 用decades强调长期争议
zoo enclosure等术语准确

提出具体标准（宽敞自然的环境）
结尾简洁有力

title: The Importance of Learning a Second Language
requirement: Learning a second language has become increasingly important in today's globalized world. Write an essay explaining the benefits of learning a second language. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
In our increasingly interconnected world, learning a second language has become more important than ever. The benefits extend far beyond simple communication.

Firstly, bilingual individuals have a competitive advantage in the job market. Many multinational companies specifically seek employees who can communicate in multiple languages. In fields such as international business, diplomacy, and tourism, language skills are often essential requirements.

Secondly, learning a new language enhances cognitive abilities. Research shows that bilingual people tend to have better problem-solving skills, improved memory, and greater mental flexibility. The process of learning a language also strengthens the brain's neural connections, which may delay the onset of dementia in older adults.

Thirdly, language learning fosters cultural understanding. When we learn another language, we gain insight into the culture, traditions, and worldview of its speakers. This promotes empathy and helps break down cultural barriers and stereotypes.

In conclusion, the benefits of learning a second language are multifaceted—professional, cognitive, and cultural. In a world that values global cooperation, being multilingual is not just an advantage but increasingly a necessity.
key_points: 双语者在就业市场有竞争优势
学习语言增强认知能力和大脑健康
语言学习促进文化理解和同理心
延缓老年人认知衰退
多语言能力是全球化时代的必需
tips: 用Firstly/Secondly/Thirdly条理清晰

从职业、认知、文化三维度展开

结尾用not just...but...升级语气

title: How to Maintain a Healthy Lifestyle
requirement: Many people struggle to maintain a healthy lifestyle due to busy schedules. Write an essay explaining practical ways to stay healthy despite a busy life. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Maintaining a healthy lifestyle can be challenging in our fast-paced world, but it is achievable with practical strategies.

The foundation of good health is a balanced diet. This means consuming plenty of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins while limiting processed foods, sugar, and excessive salt. Meal prepping on weekends can save time and ensure healthy choices throughout the week.

Regular physical activity is equally important. The World Health Organization recommends at least 150 minutes of moderate exercise per week. This doesn't require a gym membership—brisk walking, cycling, or home workouts can be just as effective. Finding activities you enjoy makes exercise sustainable.

Adequate sleep is often overlooked but crucial. Adults need 7-9 hours nightly. Establishing a consistent sleep schedule and avoiding screens before bedtime significantly improve sleep quality.

Finally, managing stress through mindfulness, meditation, or hobbies helps prevent burnout. Even 10 minutes of daily meditation can reduce anxiety and improve focus.

By integrating these habits gradually, anyone can build a healthier lifestyle without drastic changes. Small, consistent steps lead to lasting results.
key_points: 均衡饮食是健康基础——多吃蔬果全谷物
周末备餐可节省时间确保健康选择
每周至少150分钟中等强度运动
7-9小时充足睡眠至关重要
正念冥想帮助管理压力
tips: 从现实困难入手更贴近读者
每个方面给出具体可操作建议
引用WHO等权威来源
强调渐进式改变而非剧烈变化
结尾Small steps lead to results点睛

title: The Process of Photosynthesis
requirement: Photosynthesis is one of the most important biological processes on Earth. Write an essay explaining how photosynthesis works and why it is important. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Photosynthesis is the fundamental process by which plants convert sunlight into energy, sustaining virtually all life on Earth.

The process occurs primarily in plant leaves, specifically within chloroplasts containing the pigment chlorophyll. During photosynthesis, plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air through stomata and water from the soil through roots. Using energy from sunlight, chlorophyll triggers a chemical reaction that converts these raw materials into glucose and oxygen. The equation can be summarized as: carbon dioxide plus water, with light energy, produces glucose and oxygen.

Photosynthesis is critically important for several reasons. First, it produces the oxygen that all aerobic organisms, including humans, need to survive. Second, it forms the base of virtually all food chains—plants are primary producers that feed herbivores, which in turn feed carnivores. Third, photosynthesis helps regulate atmospheric carbon dioxide levels, playing a vital role in mitigating climate change.

Without photosynthesis, life as we know it would not exist. Understanding this process helps us appreciate the indispensable role plants play in maintaining Earth's ecological balance.
key_points: 光合作用将阳光转化为能量维持地球生命
发生在叶绿体中，叶绿素吸收阳光
二氧化碳+水+光能→葡萄糖+氧气
产生所有需氧生物所需的氧气
构成食物链基础，调节大气二氧化碳
tips: 用科学术语显示专业性（chloroplasts/chlorophyll）
给出化学方程式使说明更精确
从三个维度说明重要性

title: How to Prepare for a Job Interview
requirement: You will graduate soon and need to prepare for job interviews. Write an essay giving advice on how to prepare for a job interview. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Preparing for a job interview requires careful planning and practice. Here is a step-by-step guide to help you succeed.

Before the interview, research the company thoroughly. Understand its mission, products, culture, and recent news. This knowledge shows genuine interest and helps you tailor your answers. Review the job description carefully and match your skills to the requirements.

Prepare answers for common interview questions such as 'Tell me about yourself' and 'What are your strengths and weaknesses?' Use the STAR method—Situation, Task, Action, Result—to structure your responses with specific examples from your experience.

Dress professionally and arrive 10-15 minutes early. Bring extra copies of your resume, a notepad, and a pen. During the interview, maintain eye contact, speak clearly, and listen attentively. Ask thoughtful questions about the role and company to demonstrate your engagement.

After the interview, send a thank-you email within 24 hours, reiterating your interest and highlighting key points from the conversation.

With thorough preparation and confident presentation, you can make a strong impression and increase your chances of success.
key_points: 面试前深入研究公司背景和职位要求
用STAR方法结构化回答问题
着装专业，提前10-15分钟到达
面试中保持眼神交流和清晰表达
24小时内发送感谢邮件
tips: 用步骤式结构（Before/During/After）清晰
提及STAR方法等实用技巧
给出具体数字增加可操作性
从准备到后续全覆盖
结尾鼓励性总结

title: A Letter to a Friend About Studying Abroad
requirement: Your friend is considering studying abroad and has asked for your advice. Write a letter giving them advice about studying abroad. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Dear Alex,

I was thrilled to hear that you're considering studying abroad! Having spent a semester in Japan last year, I'd love to share some advice.

First, start preparing early. Research programs at least a year in advance and check credit transfer policies with your academic advisor. Apply for scholarships—many go unclaimed because students don't know about them.

Second, work on your language skills. Even basic proficiency in the local language makes daily life much easier and shows respect for the culture. I recommend language apps and conversation partners before departure.

Third, budget carefully. Beyond tuition, factor in airfare, housing, food, travel, and emergencies. I spent about $5,000 beyond regular tuition last semester. Look into part-time work options if your visa allows.

Finally, embrace the experience fully. Join local clubs, travel on weekends, and make friends from diverse backgrounds. The cultural learning outside the classroom is often as valuable as academic learning.

Studying abroad was the best decision I made in college. I'm confident it will be equally rewarding for you!

Warm regards,
Emma
key_points: 提前一年研究项目和学分转换政策
尽早申请奖学金——很多无人申领
提前学习当地语言基础
仔细预算，包括额外约5000美元开支
积极参加社团和旅行丰富体验
tips: 书信格式要完整（称呼/正文/签名）
用自己的亲身经历增加可信度
给出具体金额和时间线

title: How to Write a Research Paper
requirement: Many students find writing research papers challenging. Write a guide explaining the steps to write a good research paper. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Writing a research paper can seem daunting, but following a structured process makes it manageable.

Step 1: Choose a focused topic. Select something specific enough to cover thoroughly within your page limit. Consult your professor if unsure.

Step 2: Conduct research using credible sources. Academic databases like JSTOR and Google Scholar provide peer-reviewed articles. Aim for 8-12 sources, primarily from the last 5-10 years. Always evaluate sources for credibility and relevance.

Step 3: Create an outline with your thesis statement, main arguments, and supporting evidence. A clear outline serves as your roadmap.

Step 4: Write the draft. Start with the body paragraphs—each should focus on one main idea supported by evidence. Then write the introduction with your thesis and the conclusion summarizing your findings.

Step 5: Revise and edit. Check for logical flow, clarity, grammar, and proper citations. Reading aloud helps catch errors.

Remember, good writing is rewriting. Allow yourself time for multiple revisions, and don't hesitate to seek feedback from peers or the writing center.
key_points: 选题要具体聚焦，不宜太宽泛
使用学术数据库找同行评审文献
8-12个来源，近5-10年为主
先列大纲再写，先写正文后写首尾
好文章是改出来的，多修改
tips: 用Step 1-5步骤式结构非常清晰
每步都给出具体建议和数字
用roadmap等比喻使说明生动
结尾good writing is rewriting点题

title: An Email to a Professor Requesting a Recommendation Letter
requirement: You need a recommendation letter from a professor for graduate school. Write an email requesting one. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Dear Professor Johnson,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to request a letter of recommendation for my graduate school applications.

I was a student in your Advanced Psychology course (PSY 301) during the Fall 2024 semester, where I earned an A. I also worked as your research assistant on the cognitive behavior study, which deepened my understanding of the field.

I am applying to clinical psychology programs at three universities, with deadlines in mid-January. I would be grateful if you could write a letter highlighting my academic performance, research skills, and dedication to the field.

To assist you, I have attached my resume, transcript, and a summary of my research experience. I can also provide stamped, addressed envelopes or submit the letter electronically, whichever you prefer.

I understand you have a busy schedule, and I truly appreciate your time and consideration. If you are unable to write the letter, I completely understand. Please let me know by December 15th if possible.

Thank you for your support and mentorship throughout my undergraduate career.

Sincerely,
Sarah Chen
key_points: 邮件格式完整，语气礼貌专业
提及具体课程和成绩唤起教授记忆
说明申请项目和截止日期
主动提供简历和成绩单等辅助材料
给教授拒绝的空间，设定回复期限
tips: 邮件格式：称呼/目的/背景/请求/附件/感谢
提及具体课程和研究经历使请求有力

I truly appreciate your time and consideration

title: Changes in Urban Population (Bar Chart)
requirement: The bar chart shows the urban population growth in three cities from 2000 to 2020. Write an essay describing the data and analyzing the trends. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The bar chart illustrates the urban population changes in three cities—City A, City B, and City C—over a twenty-year period from 2000 to 2020.

In 2000, City A had the largest urban population at approximately 5 million, followed by City B at 3 million and City C at 2 million. Over the next two decades, all three cities experienced significant growth, though at different rates.

City A saw a steady increase, reaching about 7 million by 2020, representing a 40% growth rate. City B experienced the most dramatic rise, nearly doubling its population to approximately 5.8 million. This sharp increase may be attributed to rapid industrialization and migration from rural areas. City C showed moderate growth, rising to around 3.5 million by 2020.

The overall trend indicates accelerating urbanization across all three cities. City B's remarkable growth stands out, suggesting it became a major economic hub during this period. The data reflects the global pattern of urban migration driven by economic opportunities and improved urban infrastructure.

Understanding these trends is crucial for urban planning and sustainable development.

title: Student Budget Distribution (Pie Chart)
requirement: The pie chart shows how university students spend their monthly budget. Write an essay describing the data and making suggestions. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The pie chart displays the monthly budget allocation of university students across five categories.

Housing represents the largest expenditure at 35%, reflecting the high cost of accommodation near campuses. Food accounts for 25% of the budget, making it the second-largest expense. Transportation takes up 15%, which includes commuting costs and occasional travel. Entertainment and social activities constitute 10%, while textbooks and academic materials make up the remaining 15%.

Several observations can be made from this data. First, basic necessities—housing and food—consume a striking 60% of the budget, leaving limited room for discretionary spending. Second, the 15% spent on academic materials is significant and suggests that textbook costs remain a burden for many students. Universities should consider expanding open educational resources and textbook rental programs.

For students seeking to manage their budgets more effectively, sharing housing to reduce costs, cooking meals instead of eating out, and purchasing used textbooks are practical strategies. Additionally, taking advantage of student discounts for transportation can yield meaningful savings over time.

Financial literacy education could help students make more informed budgeting decisions throughout their university careers.

title: Global Temperature Rise (Line Graph)
requirement: The line graph shows the average global temperature change from 1980 to 2020. Write an essay describing the trend and discussing possible causes and effects. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The line graph depicts the average global temperature change from 1980 to 2020, revealing a clear upward trend.

In 1980, the temperature anomaly was approximately 0.2°C above the pre-industrial baseline. This figure rose gradually to about 0.4°C by 1990, then accelerated to roughly 0.7°C by 2000. The most dramatic increase occurred between 2000 and 2020, with the anomaly reaching approximately 1.1°C—a jump of 0.4°C in just two decades.

The primary cause of this warming trend is the increase in greenhouse gas emissions from human activities, particularly the burning of fossil fuels for energy and transportation. Deforestation has also contributed by reducing the planet's capacity to absorb carbon dioxide.

The consequences are already visible: melting ice caps, rising sea levels, and more frequent extreme weather events. If this trend continues, scientists project temperatures could rise by 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels as early as 2030.

Urgent action is needed to reduce emissions, transition to renewable energy, and protect forests. The data leaves no room for complacency.

主因是化石燃料排放和森林砍伐
后果包括冰盖融化、海平面上升
科学家预测2030年可能达1.5°C

title: Technology Use in Education (Table)
requirement: The table shows the percentage of schools using different technologies from 2010 to 2020. Write an essay describing the changes and analyzing the implications. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The table presents the adoption rates of four educational technologies across schools from 2010 to 2020.

Smartboards showed the highest adoption rate, rising from 30% in 2010 to 85% in 2020, making them nearly universal in modern classrooms. Laptops for students increased dramatically from 15% to 72%, reflecting the growing emphasis on one-to-one computing initiatives.

Online learning platforms experienced the most remarkable growth, surging from just 8% in 2010 to 65% in 2020. This acceleration was particularly notable after 2015, when cloud-based solutions became more accessible. Virtual reality equipment, while still the least adopted at 12% in 2020, showed promising growth from virtually 0% in 2010.

These trends indicate a fundamental transformation in educational delivery. The widespread adoption of digital tools suggests that traditional teaching methods are being significantly supplemented, if not replaced, by technology-enhanced approaches. However, the digital divide remains a concern—schools in underprivileged areas may lack the resources to keep pace.

Policymakers must ensure equitable access to educational technology to prevent widening achievement gaps between different communities.

title: The Benefits of Volunteering
requirement: Volunteering is popular among young people. Write an essay explaining the benefits of volunteering for both individuals and communities. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Volunteering has become increasingly popular among young people, and for good reason—it benefits both individuals and communities in numerous ways.

For individuals, volunteering develops valuable skills. Communication, teamwork, leadership, and problem-solving abilities are all strengthened through service activities. These transferable skills enhance employability and career prospects. Moreover, volunteers often report increased self-confidence and a greater sense of purpose, which contribute positively to mental health.

For communities, volunteers provide essential services that might otherwise be unavailable. Food banks, homeless shelters, tutoring programs, and environmental clean-ups all rely heavily on volunteer labor. Without dedicated volunteers, many community organizations would struggle to fulfill their missions.

Volunteering also bridges social divides. When people from different backgrounds work together toward a common goal, mutual understanding grows and stereotypes are challenged. This social cohesion strengthens the fabric of communities.

In conclusion, volunteering creates a virtuous cycle—individuals grow while communities flourish. I encourage everyone to find a cause they care about and dedicate even a few hours monthly. The impact, both personal and communal, is immeasurable.

title: How Climate Change Affects Biodiversity
requirement: Climate change is threatening biodiversity worldwide. Write an essay explaining how climate change affects biodiversity and what can be done to protect it. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Climate change poses one of the greatest threats to global biodiversity, disrupting ecosystems and pushing numerous species toward extinction.

Rising temperatures directly affect species' habitats. Polar bears lose sea ice hunting grounds, while coral reefs suffer from ocean warming and acidification, leading to mass bleaching events. Many species are being forced to migrate to higher altitudes or latitudes, disrupting existing ecosystems and creating competition for resources.

Changing precipitation patterns alter growing seasons and food availability. Plants may flower before their pollinators arrive, breaking critical ecological relationships. Extreme weather events—wildfires, floods, droughts—destroy habitats faster than they can recover.

To protect biodiversity, multiple strategies are needed. Reducing greenhouse gas emissions remains the most fundamental step. Expanding protected areas and creating wildlife corridors allows species to migrate safely. Restoring degraded ecosystems helps rebuild natural resilience. Additionally, captive breeding programs can prevent immediate extinctions while habitat restoration proceeds.

Protecting biodiversity is not merely an environmental concern—it is essential for human survival. We depend on healthy ecosystems for clean air, water, food, and medicine. The time for action is now.
key_points: 气温升高直接破坏物种栖息地
物种被迫迁移，破坏现有生态系统
降水模式改变打破生态关系
减少排放是最根本的措施
保护生物多样性关乎人类生存
tips: 用具体例子（北极熊、珊瑚礁）增强说服力
从温度、降水、极端天气三方面展开
解决方案分层：减排→保护区→恢复→繁殖
结尾上升到人类生存层面

title: How to Manage Your Time Effectively
requirement: Time management is a crucial skill for students. Write an essay explaining effective time management strategies. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Effective time management is essential for academic success and personal well-being. Fortunately, several proven strategies can help students make the most of their time.

The most fundamental technique is prioritization. The Eisenhower Matrix categorizes tasks into four quadrants: urgent and important, important but not urgent, urgent but not important, and neither. Focusing on important tasks before they become urgent prevents last-minute stress.

Another powerful strategy is time blocking. By dedicating specific time slots to particular tasks, students minimize distractions and maintain focus. The Pomodoro Technique—25 minutes of focused work followed by a 5-minute break—has proven especially effective for maintaining concentration over extended periods.

Additionally, breaking large assignments into smaller, manageable steps makes them less overwhelming. Instead of 'write a research paper,' start with 'create an outline' or 'find three sources.' This approach builds momentum and reduces procrastination.

Finally, learning to say no to non-essential commitments protects your time for priorities. Regular reflection on how you spend your time helps identify and eliminate inefficiencies.

Mastering time management is not about doing more—it is about doing what matters most, efficiently and consistently.
key_points: 优先排序用艾森豪威尔矩阵分类
时间块法和番茄工作法保持专注
大任务分解为小步骤减少拖延
学会拒绝不必要的承诺
时间管理是做重要的事而非做更多事
tips: 每种方法都给出具体操作建议
用Instead of...start with对比说明
结尾升华：不是做更多而是做重要的
结构清晰：四种策略各一段

title: The History of the Olympic Games
requirement: The Olympic Games have a long and fascinating history. Write an essay describing the history and significance of the Olympic Games. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The Olympic Games stand as one of humanity's most enduring traditions, spanning nearly three millennia.

The ancient Olympics began in 776 BC in Olympia, Greece, as a religious festival honoring Zeus. Athletes competed in events such as running, wrestling, and chariot racing. Only free Greek men could participate, and wars were temporarily halted during the games—a tradition known as the Olympic truce.

After being abolished in 393 AD by Roman Emperor Theodosius, the Olympics lay dormant for nearly 1,500 years. In 1896, French educator Pierre de Coubertin revived them as the modern Olympic Games in Athens, symbolizing international cooperation and athletic excellence.

Today, the Olympics have evolved into the world's largest sporting event, with over 200 nations participating. The addition of the Winter Olympics, Paralympics, and Youth Olympics reflects the movement's commitment to inclusivity.

Beyond sports, the Olympics promote cultural exchange and global unity. The Olympic motto 'Faster, Higher, Stronger—Together' encapsulates the human spirit's relentless pursuit of excellence and cooperation. Despite political controversies, the games continue to inspire millions worldwide.

比赛期间停战的奥运休战传统
1896年顾拜旦复兴现代奥运会
如今超200个国家参与
奥运精神超越体育促进全球团结
tips: 按时间顺序叙述历史脉络清晰
古代→中断→现代三段式结构

title: A Speech About Environmental Protection
requirement: You are invited to give a speech at a school event about environmental protection. Write the speech. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Good morning, fellow students and teachers.

Today I want to talk about something that affects every single one of us—our environment. We often hear about climate change and pollution, but what can we actually do?

First, reduce single-use plastics. Every year, approximately 8 million tons of plastic enter our oceans. By simply carrying a reusable water bottle and shopping bag, each of us can prevent hundreds of plastic items from reaching landfills annually.

Second, conserve energy. Turn off lights when leaving a room, unplug devices not in use, and choose walking or cycling over driving when possible. These small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, create enormous impact.

Third, support sustainable practices. Buy locally produced food to reduce transportation emissions. Choose products with minimal packaging. And most importantly, educate others—share what you learn with family and friends.

I know it can feel like individual actions don't matter. But consider this: if every person in this room commits to just one change, that's hundreds of improvements starting today.

Our planet doesn't need a few people doing it perfectly—it needs everyone doing it imperfectly. Let's start now. Thank you.

The bar chart illustrates the daily internet usage rates across five age groups in 2020.

The 18-24 age group leads with 98% daily usage, followed closely by the 25-34 group at 95%. Usage gradually declines with age: the 35-44 group shows 87%, the 45-54 group 72%, and the 55+ group 48%.

Several patterns emerge from this data. The near-universal usage among young adults reflects how deeply the internet is integrated into education, social life, and career development. The steady decline in older groups likely reflects both lower digital literacy and different lifestyle preferences.

However, the most striking insight is the 48% rate among those aged 55 and above. This is substantially higher than a decade ago, suggesting that older adults are increasingly adopting digital technologies. This trend has important implications for businesses and service providers, who must design age-friendly digital interfaces.

Furthermore, the digital divide between age groups raises concerns about equal access to online services, from healthcare appointments to government services that increasingly operate online. Bridging this gap through digital literacy programs for seniors should be a societal priority.

The table presents renewable energy investment figures for four countries over a five-year period from 2015 to 2020.

China led consistently, increasing investment from $45 billion in 2015 to $83 billion in 2020—a remarkable 84% increase. The United States ranked second, growing from $28 billion to $44 billion, a 57% rise. Germany showed steady growth from $15 billion to $24 billion, while Japan increased modestly from $12 billion to $18 billion.

Several trends stand out. China's dominance reflects strong government policy support and rapid expansion of solar and wind capacity. The US growth, though significant, has been more volatile due to shifting policy priorities. Germany's consistent investment aligns with its Energiewende policy, while Japan's lower figures may reflect limited land availability for large-scale installations.

The combined investment across all four countries nearly doubled from $100 billion to $169 billion, indicating accelerating global commitment to clean energy. However, much more investment is needed to meet international climate targets.

The data suggests that policy consistency and government commitment are key drivers of renewable energy investment.

title: Should School Uniforms Be Required?
requirement: Some schools require students to wear uniforms while others allow casual dress. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of school uniforms and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The debate over school uniforms has persisted for decades, with valid arguments on both sides.

Supporters of school uniforms argue that they promote equality by reducing visible economic differences among students. When everyone wears the same outfit, there is less pressure to wear expensive brands, which can reduce bullying and social stratification. Uniforms also foster a sense of belonging and school identity.

Opponents counter that uniforms suppress individual expression and creativity. Adolescence is a time of self-discovery, and clothing is one way young people express their identity. Additionally, uniforms can be uncomfortable and impose additional costs on families, especially those with multiple children.

In my view, a balanced approach works best. Schools could adopt a dress code rather than strict uniforms—allowing personal choice within reasonable guidelines. This maintains standards of appropriateness while respecting individual expression. Some schools have successfully implemented 'uniform colors' policies, where students choose from a palette of approved colors.

The goal should be creating an environment where students feel both included and respected as individuals. Flexibility in policy can achieve both objectives.

title: Is Homework Necessary?
requirement: Some educators believe homework is essential for learning while others think it should be reduced or eliminated. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The value of homework has been debated extensively in educational circles, with compelling arguments on both sides.

Proponents argue that homework reinforces classroom learning through practice and repetition. It helps students develop independent study habits and time management skills. Research shows that moderate homework correlates with improved academic achievement, particularly in secondary education. Homework also allows teachers to assess understanding and identify areas needing attention.

Critics, however, contend that excessive homework leads to stress, anxiety, and sleep deprivation among students. They argue that long homework hours reduce time for family, extracurricular activities, and free play—all essential for healthy development. Some studies suggest that homework has minimal benefit for elementary students.

In my opinion, the key is quality over quantity. Rather than assigning repetitive worksheets, teachers should give meaningful tasks that encourage critical thinking and creativity. A reasonable guideline might be 10 minutes per grade level per night. For a 5th grader, that means approximately 50 minutes.

Homework should enhance learning, not dominate students' lives. Thoughtful assignment design makes the difference between productive practice and pointless busywork.

title: Should We Explore Space?
requirement: Space exploration requires enormous investment. Some people think the money should be spent on Earth's problems instead. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Space exploration represents one of humanity's most ambitious endeavors, yet its enormous cost raises legitimate questions about priorities.

Supporters of space exploration point to numerous benefits. Satellite technology, developed for space programs, now enables weather forecasting, GPS navigation, and global communication. Medical innovations such as MRI technology and water purification systems originated from space research. Space exploration also inspires young people to pursue careers in science and engineering, driving technological progress.

Critics argue that the billions spent on space missions could address pressing Earth-bound problems like poverty, disease, and climate change. They question the ethics of investing in Mars missions when millions lack clean water and basic healthcare.

In my view, this is not an either-or choice. Space research and addressing terrestrial problems can coexist. Space technology frequently yields unexpected benefits for life on Earth. Furthermore, studying other planets helps us understand our own better—climate research on Venus, for instance, has advanced our knowledge of Earth's climate systems.

The challenge is maintaining a balanced investment portfolio that addresses both cosmic curiosity and earthly needs. Both are essential for humanity's future.

title: The Effects of Video Games on Children
requirement: Video games are popular among children but their effects are controversial. Discuss the positive and negative effects of video games on children and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Video games have become a dominant form of entertainment for children, sparking ongoing debate about their effects on development.

On the positive side, educational games can enhance problem-solving skills, creativity, and strategic thinking. Multiplayer games promote teamwork and cooperation. Some research indicates that action games improve hand-eye coordination and reaction times. Games like Minecraft encourage creativity and spatial reasoning, while coding games introduce children to programming concepts.

However, the negative effects cannot be dismissed. Excessive gaming is linked to sedentary lifestyles, contributing to childhood obesity. Violent games may desensitize children to aggression, though the evidence remains contested. Perhaps most concerning is gaming addiction, which the World Health Organization now recognizes as a disorder. Children who spend excessive time gaming may neglect homework, physical activity, and face-to-face social interaction.

In my opinion, the key is moderation and parental guidance. Rather than banning games entirely, parents should set reasonable time limits—perhaps one hour on school days—and ensure children play age-appropriate content. Co-playing with children allows parents to monitor content and turn gaming into a bonding activity.

Video games are tools; their impact depends on how they are used.

title: The Importance of Cultural Heritage
requirement: Cultural heritage includes traditions, monuments, and practices passed down through generations. Write an essay explaining why cultural heritage is important and how it can be preserved. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Cultural heritage represents the accumulated wisdom, traditions, and achievements of past generations. Its preservation is vital for both individual identity and societal continuity.

Cultural heritage provides communities with a sense of identity and belonging. Historical monuments, traditional crafts, and cultural practices connect people to their roots and help them understand who they are. This sense of continuity is especially important in an era of rapid globalization, where cultural homogenization threatens unique traditions.

Furthermore, cultural heritage has significant economic value through tourism. Heritage sites attract millions of visitors annually, generating income and employment for local communities. Traditional crafts and culinary traditions also contribute to cultural industries.

However, cultural heritage faces numerous threats: urbanization, armed conflict, climate change, and neglect. Preservation requires multifaceted approaches. Governments should enact protective legislation and fund restoration projects. Communities must transmit traditions to younger generations through education and cultural events. International cooperation, such as UNESCO's World Heritage program, provides crucial support.

Digital preservation—creating virtual archives and 3D models of heritage sites—offers innovative ways to safeguard cultural treasures for future generations.

Preserving our heritage is not about clinging to the past but about carrying forward the foundations upon which we build our future.

title: How the Human Immune System Works
requirement: The human immune system protects the body from disease. Write an essay explaining how the immune system works. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The human immune system is a remarkable defense network that protects the body from pathogens including bacteria, viruses, and parasites.

The immune system operates through two main mechanisms. Innate immunity provides immediate, general defense through physical barriers like skin and mucous membranes, as well as specialized cells such as phagocytes that engulf and destroy invaders. This system responds the same way to all threats.

Adaptive immunity, by contrast, provides targeted, specific responses. When the body encounters a new pathogen, white blood cells called lymphocytes produce antibodies specifically designed to neutralize it. This process takes several days during the first exposure, but the immune system remembers the pathogen. Upon subsequent exposure, it responds much faster and more effectively—this is the principle behind vaccination.

Several factors influence immune function. Adequate sleep, regular exercise, a balanced diet rich in vitamins C and D, and stress management all support immune health. Conversely, chronic stress, poor nutrition, and lack of sleep weaken immune responses.

Understanding how our immune system works empowers us to make lifestyle choices that keep this vital defense system functioning optimally.
key_points: 先天免疫提供即时通用防御（皮肤、吞噬细胞）
适应性免疫产生特异性抗体中和病原
免疫记忆原理是疫苗的基础
睡眠、运动、营养和压力管理支持免疫
慢性压力和营养不良削弱免疫功能
tips: 用专业术语但保持可读性
先先天后适应性的逻辑顺序
用vaccination联系日常生活
生活方式建议使文章更实用
结尾empowers呼应说明文目的

title: The Water Crisis and Solutions
requirement: Many regions of the world face serious water shortages. Write an essay explaining the causes of the water crisis and possible solutions. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The global water crisis affects billions of people worldwide, with scarcity threatening health, agriculture, and economic development.

Several factors drive this crisis. Population growth increases demand, while climate change alters precipitation patterns and reduces water availability in many regions. Pollution from industrial waste and agricultural runoff contaminates freshwater sources. Inefficient irrigation systems waste enormous quantities of water in agriculture, which consumes approximately 70% of global freshwater.

Solutions must address both supply and demand. On the supply side, desalination technology converts seawater to freshwater, though it remains energy-intensive. Rainwater harvesting and wastewater recycling offer more sustainable alternatives. Protecting watersheds and wetlands preserves natural water filtration systems.

On the demand side, drip irrigation reduces agricultural water use by 30-60%. Low-flow fixtures and water-efficient appliances cut household consumption. Pricing reforms that charge the true cost of water encourage conservation.

Public education is equally important. Many people are unaware of their water footprint—the total volume of freshwater used to produce the goods and services they consume. Raising awareness can drive behavioral change at both individual and institutional levels.

Addressing the water crisis requires coordinated action across governments, industries, and individuals. Every drop counts.

农业消耗70%全球淡水
海水淡化、雨水收集和废水回收
滴灌减少30-60%农业用水
水足迹意识和行为改变很重要
tips: 从原因到解决方案的清晰结构
用具体百分比增强说服力
从供给侧和需求侧两方面提方案

title: A Complaint Letter About a Product
requirement: You bought a laptop online but it has several problems. Write a complaint letter to the seller requesting a replacement or refund. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to complain about a laptop I purchased from your online store on October 1st, 2025 (Order #LN-88432).

sample_essay:
Report: Science Museum Field Trip

On Friday, October 25th, Mrs. Johnson's biology class visited the National Science Museum as part of our unit on ecosystems.

Our first stop was the Biodiversity Hall, where interactive exhibits demonstrated how different species interact within ecosystems. Students particularly enjoyed the virtual rainforest simulation, which allowed us to observe wildlife in their natural habitats through immersive technology.

Next, we attended a workshop called 'DNA and You,' where museum educators guided us through extracting DNA from strawberries. This hands-on activity reinforced our classroom learning about genetics and molecular biology. The instructors noted that our class asked particularly insightful questions.

After lunch, we explored the Climate Science exhibit, which featured real-time data visualization of global temperature patterns and their ecological impacts. Several students expressed surprise at how dramatically Arctic ice has decreased in recent decades.

The trip successfully connected textbook concepts to real-world applications. Students returned with deeper understanding and renewed enthusiasm for biology. Mrs. Johnson plans to incorporate the museum's online resources into future lessons.

Overall, this was an invaluable learning experience that we hope becomes an annual tradition.

title: Reading Habits Survey (Pie Chart + Bar Chart)
requirement: The charts show the results of a survey about students' reading habits, including preferred formats and genres. Write an essay describing the data and discussing the implications. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The charts present findings from a survey of 1,000 university students regarding their reading habits, covering both preferred formats and genres.

Regarding format, e-books have become the most popular choice at 42%, surpassing printed books at 35%. Audiobooks account for 15%, while academic journals make up the remaining 8%. This shift toward digital formats reflects the convenience of carrying entire libraries on a single device.

Genre preferences reveal interesting patterns. Fiction leads at 40%, followed by academic texts at 25%, self-help at 18%, and news articles at 17%. The relatively high percentage of academic reading is expected among university students, but the strong preference for fiction suggests that reading remains a leisure activity, not merely an academic obligation.

The data raises important considerations for educators and publishers. While digital formats offer convenience, research suggests that reading comprehension may be better with printed materials for complex texts. The popularity of fiction also presents an opportunity—teachers could leverage students' love of storytelling to introduce academic concepts through narrative approaches.

Understanding these reading habits helps tailor educational strategies to contemporary student preferences.

虽然数字化便利但纸质更适合深度理解
可利用故事化方式引入学术概念

title: How Robot Assistants Have Changed Daily Life
requirement: Robot Assistants have become common in daily life. Write an essay explaining how Robot Assistants have changed the way people live and work. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The widespread adoption of Robot Assistants has fundamentally transformed daily life in numerous ways.

In the workplace, Robot Assistants have enhanced productivity and flexibility. Remote work has become feasible for many professions, allowing employees to work from anywhere with an internet connection. Communication has become instantaneous, enabling real-time collaboration across time zones. Tasks that once required physical presence can now be completed digitally.

In personal life, Robot Assistants have reshaped how people communicate, access information, and entertain themselves. Social connections are maintained through messaging and video calls, while news and entertainment are available on demand. However, this constant connectivity can blur the boundary between work and personal time, leading to burnout.

Health implications are also notable. While Robot Assistants provide access to fitness tracking and health information, excessive screen time contributes to eye strain, poor posture, and reduced physical activity.

Looking ahead, responsible use of Robot Assistants will determine whether they enhance or diminish our quality of life. Setting boundaries, practicing digital detox, and prioritizing face-to-face interactions can help maintain a healthy relationship with technology.

Technology should serve us, not the reverse.
key_points: Robot Assistant使远程办公和即时通讯成为可能
即时通讯促进跨时区协作
但模糊了工作与生活的界限
屏幕时间过长影响健康
负责任的使用决定技术影响
tips: 从工作到个人生活两大维度展开
用However转折引出问题
用Looking ahead展望未来
结尾用Technology should serve us有力总结

title: The Rise of Organic Food
requirement: Organic Food has become increasingly popular. Write an essay explaining the reasons for its popularity and its effects on society. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The growing popularity of Organic Food reflects significant changes in modern lifestyles and food culture.

Several factors drive this trend. Convenience is perhaps the most significant—busy work schedules and fast-paced lives leave many people with little time for meal preparation. Organic Food offers a quick and accessible solution. Affordability also plays a role, as Organic Food is often less expensive than traditional alternatives. Additionally, globalization has exposed consumers to diverse culinary traditions, increasing demand for variety.

However, the effects on society are mixed. On one hand, Organic Food provides employment opportunities and stimulates economic activity in the food industry. On the other hand, health concerns cannot be ignored. If Organic Food replaces balanced, nutritious meals, it may contribute to dietary imbalances and related health issues.

The key lies in moderation and informed choices. Consumers should understand both the benefits and drawbacks of Organic Food and make dietary decisions that balance convenience with nutrition. Public health campaigns and clearer nutritional labeling can help people make more informed choices.

As our food culture continues evolving, awareness and balance remain essential.
key_points: Organic Food流行反映现代生活方式变化
便利和可负担是主要驱动因素
全球化促进饮食多样化
但健康隐患不容忽视
关键在适度选择和营养均衡
tips: 从原因到影响的清晰结构
用On one hand/On the other hand平衡论述
用moderation and informed choices概括核心建议
提及公共卫生和标签等政策建议
结尾简短呼应主题

title: Should Economics Be a Required Subject?
requirement: Some people think Economics should be required for all students, while others disagree. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The question of whether Economics should be a required subject has generated considerable debate among educators and parents alike.

Supporters argue that Economics develops important skills and knowledge that benefit students regardless of their career path. A well-rounded education includes exposure to diverse disciplines, and Economics contributes uniquely to students' intellectual and personal development. Furthermore, requiring Economics ensures that students who might not choose it voluntarily still benefit from its study.

Opponents counter that making Economics mandatory reduces students' ability to explore subjects aligned with their interests and career goals. With limited time in the curriculum, required courses may displace electives that students are more passionate about. Additionally, not all students have the same aptitude for every subject.

In my view, Economics should be offered as a strongly recommended elective rather than a strict requirement. This approach encourages participation while respecting student choice. Schools could also integrate Economics concepts into interdisciplinary projects, ensuring exposure without mandating standalone courses.

Flexibility in curriculum design serves students best.
key_points: Economics培养重要技能和知识
通识教育需要多元学科
但强制减少学生选择自由
建议作为强烈推荐选修而非必修
可通过跨学科项目确保接触
tips: 用both views结构保持论证平衡
用well-rounded education等术语
提出elective vs requirement的折中方案
提及interdisciplinary projects创新解决
结尾简洁有力

title: University Research Funding Trends (Line Graph)
requirement: The line graph shows university Research Funding from 2015 to 2025. Write an essay describing the trends and analyzing the possible causes. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The line graph illustrates changes in university Research Funding over a ten-year period from 2015 to 2025.

In 2015, Research Funding stood at approximately 25 percent. This figure remained relatively stable until 2018, after which a noticeable upward trend emerged. By 2021, Research Funding had risen to roughly 55 percent, and the growth accelerated further, reaching approximately 48 percent by 2025.

Several factors likely contributed to this trend. The increasing reputation of the university may have attracted more interest and investment. Changes in government policy supporting higher education could also have played a role. Additionally, the launch of new programs and facilities during this period may have stimulated growth.

The acceleration after 2020 is particularly noteworthy. This may reflect a post-pandemic recovery or the impact of recent strategic initiatives. The university's expansion of online programs could also have broadened its reach.

If this trajectory continues, Research Funding is projected to increase further over the next five years. The administration should plan accordingly to ensure that growth is supported by adequate infrastructure and resources.

Sustainable growth requires proactive planning.
key_points: 2015年Research Funding约25 percent
2018年后明显上升趋势
2021年增至约35 percent
2020年后加速可能反映疫后复苏
需提前规划确保可持续增长

From: Student Council
Date: December 2025

Background:
A recent survey of 600 students revealed that 78% believe our campus needs a modern Gym. Currently, students must travel off-campus for Gym services, which is inconvenient and time-consuming.

Benefits:
1. Academic Enhancement: A campus Gym would provide students with convenient access to resources and activities that support their academic and personal development.
2. Community Building: The Gym would serve as a gathering space, fostering connections among students from different programs and backgrounds.
3. Recruitment Advantage: Modern facilities help attract prospective students, enhancing the university's competitive position.

Proposed Location and Budget:
The empty lot adjacent to the Student Center would be an ideal location. Preliminary cost estimates range from $500,000 to $800,000, with potential for phased construction. We suggest seeking alumni donations and government grants to offset costs.

Timeline:
If approved by March 2026, construction could begin by September and be completed within 18 months.

We respectfully urge the administration to consider this proposal for the benefit of the entire campus community.

title: University Research Funding Trends (Line Graph)
requirement: The line graph shows university Research Funding from 2015 to 2025. Write an essay describing the trends and analyzing the possible causes. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The line graph illustrates changes in university Research Funding over a ten-year period from 2015 to 2025.

In 2015, Research Funding stood at approximately 25 thousands. This figure remained relatively stable until 2018, after which a noticeable upward trend emerged. By 2021, Research Funding had risen to roughly 55 thousands, and the growth accelerated further, reaching approximately 48 thousands by 2025.

Several factors likely contributed to this trend. The increasing reputation of the university may have attracted more interest and investment. Changes in government policy supporting higher education could also have played a role. Additionally, the launch of new programs and facilities during this period may have stimulated growth.

The acceleration after 2020 is particularly noteworthy. This may reflect a post-pandemic recovery or the impact of recent strategic initiatives. The university's expansion of online programs could also have broadened its reach.

If this trajectory continues, Research Funding is projected to increase further over the next five years. The administration should plan accordingly to ensure that growth is supported by adequate infrastructure and resources.

Sustainable growth requires proactive planning.

2018年后明显上升趋势
2021年增至约35 thousands
2020年后加速可能反映疫后复苏
需提前规划确保可持续增长

title: The Rise of Organic Food
requirement: Organic Food has become increasingly popular. Write an essay explaining the reasons for its popularity and its effects on society. Write 120-180 words.
sample_essay:
The growing popularity of Organic Food reflects significant changes in modern lifestyles and food culture.

Several factors drive this trend. Convenience is perhaps the most significant—busy work schedules and fast-paced lives leave many people with little time for meal preparation. Organic Food offers a quick and accessible solution. Affordability also plays a role, as Organic Food is often less expensive than traditional alternatives. Additionally, globalization has exposed consumers to diverse culinary traditions, increasing demand for variety.

However, the effects on society are mixed. On one hand, Organic Food provides employment opportunities and stimulates economic activity in the food industry. On the other hand, health concerns cannot be ignored. If Organic Food replaces balanced, nutritious meals, it may contribute to dietary imbalances and related health issues.

The key lies in moderation and informed choices. Consumers should understand both the benefits and drawbacks of Organic Food and make dietary decisions that balance convenience with nutrition. Public health campaigns and clearer nutritional labeling can help people make more informed choices.

As our food culture continues evolving, awareness and balance remain essential.
key_points: Organic Food流行反映现代生活方式变化
便利和可负担是主要驱动因素
全球化促进饮食多样化
但健康隐患不容忽视
关键在适度选择和营养均衡
tips: 从原因到影响的清晰结构
用On one hand/On the other hand平衡论述
用moderation and informed choices概括核心建议
提及公共卫生和标签等政策建议
结尾简短呼应主题
